description_long: This house features a single-story layout with a broad, rectangular footprint and a low-pitched, red-tiled hip roof. The exterior walls are finished in a combination of yellow and beige tones, with a prominent front entryway that includes a covered porch supported by two dark columns and a small set of steps. Windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical row along the longer side, with a few asymmetrically placed on the shorter facades. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, with tiled roofing and glass windows. The main entrance is accentuated by a projecting canopy and a paved landing, providing a welcoming focal point. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of suburban or urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story house with a red-tiled hip roof, yellow and beige exterior walls, and a covered front porch with columns. Windows are arranged in rows, and the main entryway features a small set of steps and a canopy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a covered porch, two dark columns, and a small set of steps, as well as windows and a mix of yellow and beige walls.
description_view_1: Rear-side view highlighting the expanse of the red-tiled roof and the plain, windowless brown wall, with minimal architectural features visible.
description_view_2: Back view displaying a row of windows along the yellow wall and a side entrance, with the roof and wall colors clearly visible.
description_view_3: Angled view of the side and front, showing the yellow wall with multiple windows and the prominent front porch with its canopy and columns.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with some asymmetrical placement
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with two dark columns, small set of steps, projecting canopy, and a single main door
